data.title: Casella Waste Systems to remarket $29M in Maine Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds, $1M to be redeemed on Aug 1.
data.description: RUTLAND, Vermont, July 17, 2025 - Casella Waste Systems, Inc., a company specializing in regional solid waste, recycling, and resource management services, has announced the commencement of the remarketing of $29.0 million aggregate principal amount of Finance Authority of Maine Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds. These bonds, referred to as the Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Project, consist of Series 2015R-1 issued on August 27, 2015, and Series 2015R-2 issued on April 2, 2018, with an initial total issuance of $30.0 million. Casella plans to redeem $1.0 million of the bonds on August 1, 2025, while the remaining $29.0 million will be remarketed on the same date as the "Remarketed Bonds".

data.title: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. is remarketing $37.5 million of NY Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds it previously issued.
data.description: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. has initiated the remarketing of $37.5 million of New York State Environmental Facilities Corporation Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds Series 2020R-1, originally issued for $40.0 million on September 2, 2020. $2.5 million of the Bonds will be redeemed by Casella on September 2, 2025, with the remaining $37.5 million to be remarketed on the same date. Casella, a regional company offering solid waste, recycling, and resource management services, made the announcement on August 18, 2025. This move aims to manage and reallocate these financial resources effectively.

data.title: Article highlights Derek Bryson Park's financial and diplomatic leadership journey, transitioning from Vatican diplomat to Wall Street innovator.
data.description: The remarkable journey of Derek Bryson Park, once a Vatican diplomat, now a leading innovator on Wall Street, reveals his significant global impact. data.title: PYSFX provides diversified exposure to securitized credit, catering to income investors looking for timely strategies.
data.description: The Payden Securitized Income Fund, known by its ticker symbol PYSFX, provides income investors with a diversified portfolio of securitized credit assets. This fund aims to offer a range of investment opportunities within the securitized credit market, thus allowing investors to access a variety of income-generating assets. PYSFX is designed to provide investors with exposure to securitized credit while seeking to generate a competitive level of income. By investing in PYSFX, individuals can benefit from the potential income opportunities presented by securitized credits.

data.title: Wall Street hits record highs on Fed rate cut bets despite economic concerns. S&P 500 up 0.8% to 6,584, Nasdaq 100 up 0.7% over 24,000, Dow Jones up 1.3% to 46,080. Investors expect a 25-basis-point rate cut next week.
data.description: All three major U.S. stock market indices - S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, and Dow Jones - reached new all-time highs on Thursday as investors showed confidence in riskier assets on the belief that interest rates may decrease soon. This spike occurred despite concerning economic indicators: U.S. inflation rose to 2.9% year-on-year in August, and weekly jobless claims hit 263,000, the highest since October 2021. However, investors seemed unconcerned about potential stagflation, trusting that the Federal Reserve would prioritize stabilizing the weakening job market over controlling inflation. By midday in New York, the S&P 500 rose by 0.8% to 6,584, the Nasdaq 100 went up by 0.7% surpassing 24,000, and the Dow Jones surged by 1.3% to 46,080. Speculators have fully factored in a 25-basis-point rate cut expected next week.
Wag! Group Co. finishes Chapter 11 plan overhaul efficiently.

data.title: Golden Triangle Ventures appoints Javier Leal as CEO after governance transition, advancing growth strategy.
data.description: Golden Triangle Ventures, Inc. (OTC: GTVH) based in Las Vegas, completed its governance transition on August 27, 2025. Javier Leal was appointed as the Chief Executive Officer as per the previously disclosed Memorandum of Understanding (MOU). This transition offers the company unified leadership and clear accountability to carry out its growth strategy effectively.

data.title: Elon Musk offered $97.4 billion to buy parent company of ChatGPT, facing legal battles with OpenAI CEO. Vyome reviewing strategic alternatives for Livechain. Peraso Inc considering strategic options, GTCR's $627M buyout under antitrust scrutiny. Cenovus Energy agreed to buy MEG Energy.
data.description: Elon Musk sought a $97.4 billion offer from Mark Zuckerberg to purchase the parent company of ChatGPT, but neither Zuckerberg nor Meta Platforms, Inc. signed a letter of intent. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's rivalry with Musk over AI continues, leading OpenAI to request a court order for Meta to produce documents related to Musk's takeover bid. Vyome is exploring strategic options for Livechain Inc., which it acquired in 2021. Mercuries Life Insurance in Taiwan is considering a potential sale or stake sale. Peraso Inc., maker of mmWave wireless tech, is seeking strategic options, but potential acquirer Mobix Labs wants direct access to the board. GTCR's $627 million buyout of Surmodics Inc. is facing antitrust scrutiny. Cenovus Energy agreed to purchase Canadian oil sands company MEG Energy. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Blade Air Mobility to change name to Strata Critical Medical, trading under SRTA ticker symbol. Focus on medical services.
data.description: Blade Air Mobility, Inc. (Nasdaq: BLDE) has confirmed its legal name change to Strata Critical Medical, Inc. The shift, set to happen this week, signifies its strategic pivot towards offering essential logistic and medical support to hospitals and healthcare entities nationwide. This move follows the finalization of an agreement to sell Blade’s Passenger division to Joby Aviation, Inc. (NYSE: JOBY). This new focus reflects the Company's determination to cater to critical medical needs in the industry.

data.title: Miran dissents on Fed interest rate cut; Warren accuses him as 'Trump's sock puppet.' First FOMC meeting, only dissenting vote.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran dissented during a recent FOMC meeting by voting for a half-percentage-point interest rate cut, in contrast to the Fed's decision for a 25 basis point cut. Miran, who was appointed last week, faced criticism from economists and politicians, including Senator Elizabeth Warren, who accused him of furthering President Trump's agenda within the Federal Reserve. Miran's dissent has sparked controversy, with Warren calling him Trump's "sock puppet" at the Fed. This move has stirred mixed reactions and raised concerns about political influence within the central bank. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
